title: Miss WEIO 2012
summary: CIRI descendant Rosemary “Utuana” Berg was crowned Miss WEIO in the 2012 World Eskimo- Indian Olympics (WEIO) Pageant. title: In memory, September 2012
summary: Gertrude “Gertie” Maluweena Ah’Nee, 73 Gertrude Ahnee passed away May 30. Ms. Ah’nee was born June 25, 1938, in Shishmaref, Alaska. title: North Wind recognized nationally
summary: Engineering News Record named North Wind, a CIRI company, a top environmental firm for the fourth consecutive year. summary: In honor of civil rights leader Elizabeth Peratrovich, volunteer lawyers will assist the Alaska Federation of Natives convention attendees by appointment with civil legal issues involving family law, difficulties with receiving public benefits, safe housing, consumer issues and completing estate pla...

title: Cook Inlet Lending Center earns top award
summary: Cook Inlet Lending Center (CILC) was awarded $1,453,806 in August by the Community Development Financial Institutions Fund (CDFI Fund), a nonprofit lending enterprise of Cook Inlet Housing Authority. title: TCF issues 2012-2013 academic year awards
summary: The CIRI Foundation (TCF) recently awarded more than 430 individual scholarship and grant awards totaling nearly $2 million. title: Koahnic recognized
summary: Koahnic Broadcast Corp.’s (KBC) programs earned first, second and third place in the Broadcast Division by the Native American Journalists Association (NAJA) at the UNITY Journalists Convention in Las Vegas on Aug. 3. “Native America Calling” received top honors with the first place award. “National...

title: CIRI Spotlight:  Jaclyn Sallee
summary: CIRI shareholder Jaclyn Sallee’s interest in broadcasting and developing relevant programming for Alaskans was set early. As a child, Sallee only spoke English and wanted to connect with her grandmother who only spoke Inupiat. One of Sallee’s childhood memories was of her grandmother Lena Ahnangnatoguk listening to the radio or watching television programs she could not understand because they were not available in her Native language. Lena Ahnangnatoguk was a strong influence...

title: CIRI signs exploration agreement with Apache
summary: CIRI and Apache Corp. signed an agreement to explore for oil and natural gas on CIRI lands in the Cook Inlet basin. “CIRI is on board with moving resource development forward in Southcentral Alaska,” said Margie Brown, CIRI president and CEO. “Responsible use of resource-rich lands will benefit Alas...

title: A word from the president:  Changing the economic landscape
summary: Why Fire Island Wind means so much to CIRI By: Margie Brown, CIRI president and chief executive officer In this issue you will read that CIRI’s Fire Island Wind project is connected to Southcentral Alaska’s electric grid and producing power. Now I will use the completion of this project to illuminate (no pun intended) what Fire Island Wind means to CIRI and why it is important to Alaskans in general. title: Sam’s Club coming to Tikahtnu Commons
summary: Workers broke ground and started construction on the new Sam’s Club at Tikahtnu Commons in August. summary: Mark your calendars: CIRI and The CIRI Foundation’s annual Friendship Potlatches are nearing!
